heroSubtitle: Site feasibility for any Chicago address. In seconds.
heroSubline: Zoning, incentives, tax projections, comparable sales, and municipal code analysis — all in one free Scorecard.

hero.askAnalystLead: Researching the code or a neighborhood?

intelligence.heading: One question. Twenty-five sources.
intelligence.subheading: Zoning, municipal code, property records, incentives, permits, and more — queried simultaneously.

story.feasibilitySubtitle: Zoning classification, regulatory overlays, incentive eligibility, tax projections, and comparable sales — assembled from 25+ sources for any Chicago address. The due diligence that used to take a week.
story.reportSubtitle: Ask a question in plain English. Get a sourced analysis with cited municipal code, interactive maps, and trend data — then get the full Development Feasibility Report, a professional PDF you can hand to a client, investor, or lender. $25 per parcel.

depth.heading: Ask one question. Get the full picture.
depth.subheading: Here's what UrbanLayer returns when you ask about a single Chicago address.

cards.title: Property Scorecard
cards.caption: Parcel data, 5-year assessment history, sales records, and tax estimates from Cook County.

cards.title: Regulatory profile
cards.caption: 12 overlay layers checked per address — landmarks to flood zones to ADU eligibility.

cards.title: Site context
cards.caption: Demographics, transit access, and walkability scores assembled for every query.

personas.heading: Built for real decisions
personas.subheading: Whether you're evaluating a development site, checking code compliance, or researching incentive eligibility.

items.title: The Developer
items.question: Can I build a 6-unit residential building at 4520 N Clark St?
items.framing: Complete site feasibility in seconds, not days.

items.title: The Architect
items.question: What are the setback requirements for a mixed-use building in B3-2 adjacent to an RS-3 district?
items.framing: Every answer cites the specific code section.

items.title: The Attorney
items.question: Is the property at 1601 N Milwaukee Ave eligible for a Class 6b tax incentive?
items.framing: Due diligence research in one query, not ten FOIA requests.

howItWorks.heading: How it works

steps.title: Enter an address
steps.description: Start with the property — type any Chicago address and the parcel's Scorecard opens instantly. No account, no forms.

steps.title: We assemble the Scorecard
steps.description: City, county, federal, and legal sources are queried in parallel — zoning, overlays, incentives, taxes, and comparable sales.

steps.title: You get the Scorecard
steps.description: A free Scorecard assessment in seconds, an AI analyst for cited follow-up questions — and the full Development Feasibility Report ($25) when you need the deliverable.

howItWorks.sampleResponse: The parcel at 2100 N Damen Ave is zoned

valueProps.buildBody: Instant zoning classification with AI-powered code interpretation. Ask what's allowed, what the setbacks are, what FAR applies — get cited answers from 14,000+ municipal code sections.
valueProps.worthBody: Assessment history, comparable sales, PTAXSIM tax projections, and incentive eligibility. The financial picture for any Chicago address.
valueProps.watchBody: Regulatory overlays, environmental flags, building violations, and permit history. The risks and opportunities you need to see before committing capital.

footer.tagline: AI-powered site feasibility for Chicago real estate professionals.
footer.cookCountyDesc: Property Characteristics, Assessed Values, Sales History, Tax Estimation
footer.federalExternalDesc: Census Demographics, FEMA Flood Zones, EPA Brownfields, Walk Score
footer.notAffiliated: Not affiliated with the City of Chicago
footer.dataDelay: Data may be delayed up to 7 days
